setting.note: Archbishop Latour's New Mexico Territory of the 1850s, from the plaza and the cathedral he raises at Santa Fé, out to the sky-city of Ácoma on its mesa, up to rebel Taos and down through Albuquerque. Cather names the real pueblos, towns and missions; the bbox spans northern New Mexico from Ácoma and Albuquerque up to Taos.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Willa Cather, first published 1927; the author died in 1947.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US (published 1927) and the UK/EU (author died 1947).
rights.attribution: Willa Cather, Death Comes for the Archbishop (1927). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The carnelian red of the New Mexico earth, the hills that ring Santa Fé and rise behind the cathedral, taken straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.
